{# Furo renders pages through base.html (it does not support layout.html), so SEO meta tags are added by overriding its empty extrahead block. Only the homepage carries the site-level description and the SoftwareApplication structured data. If every page emitted them, all pages would advertise the same generic description (duplicate meta descriptions, which search engines discount) and each would claim to be the application's landing page (misplaced schema.org data that muddies rich results and AI overviews). Content pages instead expose per-page Open Graph/Twitter tags and let search engines derive the snippet from the page body. #} {% extends "!base.html" %} {% block extrahead %} {{ super() }} {% if pagename == "index" %} {# Structured data describes the software as a whole, so it belongs only on the landing page. #} {% else %} {% endif %} {% endblock %}
